name: Flutter MCP Server
what_it_does: Flutter MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Flutter mobile and cross-platform app development — querying widget trees, accessing Flutter DevTools data, running Flutter commands, managing project structure, and integrating Flutter's cross-platform development toolkit into agent-driven mobile app development workflows.
use_cases: Querying Flutter widget tree structure from UI debugging agents
Accessing Flutter DevTools performance data from profiling agents
Running flutter build and test commands from development automation agents
Inspecting Dart code and project structure from code analysis agents
AI-assisted Flutter UI development from pair programming agents
Debugging Flutter app state from troubleshooting agents
not_for: Non-Flutter mobile development (React Native, native iOS/Android require different tools)
Teams without Flutter SDK and Dart installed
best_when: A Flutter developer needs AI assistance with cross-platform app development — querying widget trees, running commands, and integrating Flutter tooling into agent workflows.
avoid_when: You're using React Native, native iOS/Android, or other mobile frameworks — Flutter-specific tools only work with Flutter.

auth.notes: No authentication — local MCP interacting with Flutter toolchain. Flutter SDK must be installed on host.

pricing.notes: Flutter SDK is free. MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: Local Flutter toolchain — limited by build/test execution speed

known_agent_gotchas: Flutter SDK must be installed and in PATH — separate installation required
DevTools integration may require specific Flutter version
Widget tree inspection requires running app with DevTools connected
Community MCP from Arenukvern — limited documentation and support
Flutter build times can be significant — agents must handle long operations
Dart and Flutter API knowledge required for effective tool use
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local Flutter toolchain. No credentials. Community MCP. No external data exposure. Standard local development security applies.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local Flutter toolchain. No credentials. Community MCP. No external exposure.
